message:
feat(recorder_migration): Cx-first m2_import (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to tick 67. Second of 4 MigrationEngine async entry
points.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `MigrationEngine::m2_import_with_cx(cx, target, records,
    manifest)` — Cx-first parallel of `m2_import`. Checkpoints
    at 2 seams:
      - pre-flight: pre-cancelled cx returns before any target
        writes.
      - per-chunk inside the chunk-import loop: cx.checkpoint()?
        at the top of each `records.chunks(import_batch_size)`
        iteration with the running `import_count` embedded in
        the cancellation error.

Cancellation-safety contract (documented): a mid-import cancel
leaves the target with all successfully-committed chunks intact.
The `batch_id` idempotency (format: "consumer_id-first_ord-last_ord")
means a subsequent resume will skip already-written chunks
cleanly — no data duplication.

Verification semantics on cancellation: the digest + count
verification runs ONLY after the loop completes naturally. A
cancellation short-circuits before the verification checks,
which is the right behavior — a partial import's digest will
not match the full-export digest, so running the verification
would produce a spurious DigestMismatch error on top of the
cancellation. By returning the TargetWriteError cancellation
early, the caller sees a single, accurate error indicating what
happened.

Error mapping: cancellation wraps into
`MigrationError::TargetWriteError` (same reasoning as tick 67's
SourceDegraded mapping — preserves `Result` signature without
introducing a new error variant). The message starts with
"cancelled" for pattern-matching.

Regression tests (2 new):

  * `m2_import_with_cx_matches_legacy` — 3-record fixture with
    import_batch_size=2 (forces 2 chunks — exercises the
    per-chunk checkpoint seam). Asserts total events written,
    import_count, import_digest, and digest-matches-export
    parity.

  * `m2_import_with_cx_digest_mismatch_aborts` — tampered
    manifest.export_digest (set to 0xDEADBEEF) must produce a
    DigestMismatch error on the Cx path just like the legacy
    path does. Guards against a regression where the
    verification branches might be bypassed on the Cx path
    (e.g. if someone accidentally mixed up where the checkpoint
    fires relative to the verification).

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * recorder_migration:: — 52 passed / 0 failed (tick 67: 50 ->
    tick 68: 52, +2 new parity tests; all 6 legacy m2 tests
    still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (45.12s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first recorder_migration.rs progress after tick 68:
  * `m0_preflight`       ✓ (tick 67)
  * `m2_import`          ✓ (tick 68, this commit)
  * `m3_checkpoint_sync` — pending
  * `m5_cutover`         — pending
  * `run_m0_m2`          — pending (composite)

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
